name: Keyboard Formatter

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Minor tweaks to comply to [Plugin guidelines - Developer Documentation](https://docs.obsidian.md/Plugins/Releasing/Plugin+guidelines):

- Use sentence case in UI
- Use obsidian's headings
- Removed console logging

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Lauloque/Keyboard-Formatter/compare/1.2.0...1.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- refactor using obsidian HTML elements and style.css to comply with [requirements](https://github.com/obsidianmd/obsidian-releases/pull/6416#issuecomment-2998734162)
- added numpad keys support
- changed delete key icon direction

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Lauloque/Keyboard-Formatter/compare/1.1.0...1.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Removed default shortcut as per official [requirement](https://github.com/obsidianmd/obsidian-releases/pull/6416#issuecomment-2888036952):

> [[1](https://github.com/Lauloque/Obsidian-Keyboard-Formatter/blob/79b5b0f9eb454bd1734f95cc7275c620848c6a04/main.ts#L8)]:We recommend against providing a default hotkey when possible. The reason being that it's easy to pick a hotkey that a user already has configured and they could get confused when that key doesn't do what they expect. Also, it's hard choosing a safe default hotkey that's available for all operating systems.
